validation_summary.evidence: python -m py_compile exits 0 (~0.1s); python -m unittest skills.development-harness.scripts.tests.test_skill_md_version_upgrades -v passes 8/8 (0.002s); python -m unittest discover skills/development-harness/scripts/tests passes 311/312 with 1 OS-specific skip (38.2s, up from 304 -- 8 new cases for unit_052)

checkpoint.summary: unit_052 complete. skills/development-harness/SKILL.md gained a new 'Version upgrades' section placed between 'Python Detection' and 'Key Principle'. Three numbered steps: (1) Re-run /create-development-harness when schema_version bumps; (2) ROADMAP.md + PHASES/*.md are preserved (the recreate flow reads them and rebuilds around them -- real product/planning work is never regenerated from scratch); (3) Lists the 4 core regenerated files (state.json, phase-graph.json, checkpoint.md, manifest.json) + scripts + workspace commands + hook/rule files so users with hand-edits know those edits will be lost. Followed by the load-bearing paragraph 'No migration script is provided by design' verbatim (per acceptance criterion). Cost argument named explicitly + cross-link to config.execution_mode.versioning.break_on_schema_bump (the break->true default from unit_048) as the config knob that enforces the policy. Users who want in-place migration attempts can flip to break_on_schema_bump: false but know upfront that the harness ships no migration tooling. New test_skill_md_version_upgrades.py (150 LOC, 8 cases across 3 classes) pins heading + position-order (before Key Principle so readers find it on schema-mismatch errors) + 5 acceptance-criterion tokens (Re-run + /create-development-harness, ROADMAP.md + PHASES/*.md + preserved, verbatim 'no migration script is provided by design' lowercase match, cost argument, break_on_schema_bump config mention) + regenerated-files listing so hand-edit users aren't surprised. Suite 304 -> 312.
checkpoint.next_action: Complete unit_053 (PHASE_012): update skills/development-harness/commands/update.md and skills/development-harness/templates/workspace-commands/update-development-harness.md to detect schema_version mismatch between the installed .harness/ and the installed skill; emit a pointer to /create-development-harness with a one-line explanation; do not attempt auto-migration. Validation: both docs instruct the mismatch detection + pointer behavior; v1 fixture test asserts the message is emitted via python -m unittest skills.development-harness.scripts.tests.test_update_command. This is the LAST unit of PHASE_012 -- after it lands, open the phase PR, run code-review, squash-merge per autonomous-merge authorization.
